seo.og_description: We compared Descope and Logto head-to-head. Pricing: Free – $2,000/mo vs Free – $599/mo. Read our full analysis.

hero.headline: Descope vs Logto for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Descope and Logto — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Descope vs Logto is a common comparison we hear from Next.js developers. Descope (4.2★) and Logto (4.1★) both have loyal followings. Our analysis shows Descope has the edge for most Next.js projects due to its 7/10 DX score. Logto is a strong alternative if yes, up to 5,000 mau better matches your budget.
verdict_text: Descope is my pick for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 20 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Logto is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, up to 5,000 mau or prefer Logto's approach to authentication.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, up to 5,000 MAU with 2,000 transactions

pros: Visual drag-and-drop workflow builder for authentication flows
No-code screens reduce front-end development time
Built-in analytics and user journey tracking
Support for passkeys, biometrics, and passwordless auth
cons: Expensive at scale - pricing jumps at 10K MAU
Visual builder adds abstraction that can be limiting for custom flows
Smaller community and less third-party integration

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

faq.question: Should I choose Descope or Logto for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Descope scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Descope because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 5,000 MAU with 2,000 transactions or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Descope or Logto more affordable?
faq.answer: Descope starts at Free – $2,000/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU with 2,000 transactions. Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Descope or Logto easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Descope has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Descope and Logto together?
faq.answer: Using both Descope and Logto in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Descope or Logto?
faq.answer: Descope has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.2★. Logto has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.1★. On paper, Descope leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
